textSpans.text: The patient is a 77 year old female who presents for a lower extremity evaluation.
The patient is transitioning into care from another physician and a summary of care was not provided.
Patient presents to clinic today with problems related to his right leg.
The patient is 77 years old and we discussed the fact that I think he has a sarcoma and that chemotherapy is not a good
A total of 50 minutes was spent in direct contact with the patient interviewing the patient, examining the patient, reviewing
"BMI >25: Losing Weight" handout given to address weight-loss options and benefits.
HEENT Not Present- Hearing Loss, Hoarseness, Visual Disturbances and Wears glasses/contact lenses.
Once tissue diagnosis has been obtained then we can decide whether radiation therapy will be necessary for local
We discussed the fact that the differential diagnosis would be an infection
Pathological fracture of fibula, initial encounter ( M84.469A)
Malignant neoplasm of right fibula ( C40.21)
M84.469A) Malignant neoplasm of right fibula ( C40
He had an episode where he bumped his leg recently and had onset of pain.
Contract: No Active Pain Contract Phreesia 03/09/2026
Cardiovascular Not Present- Chest Pain, Difficulty Breathing On Exertion, Palpitations, Shortness of Breath and Swelling of
Head, ears, eyes, nose, and throat: Atraumatic and normocephalic.
Alert and oriented to person, place, time, and situation.
We discussed the general behavior of sarcomas.
Psychiatric Not Present- Anxiety, Depression, Fearful, Frequent crying and Other Psychological Condition.
Skin: No open lesions, rashes or wounds.
There is no break in the skin.
In anticipation of surgery he is given a letter requesting cardiac risk stratification.

referralDashboardSections.label: Plan of Care and Physical Therapy Evaluation

textSpans.text: The condition involves the right lower extremity.
Transition into care is described as the following:
He has chronic lymphocytic leukemia which has been
diagnosed and it was found to be within the lesion.
Subsequent imaging has been completed and he wishes to be evaluated
for the etiology and treatment options for the lesion.
He has an aching sensation in the leg with weightbearing.
He is at his baseline level of health otherwise.
No dark or hairy patches present.
Vascular: Normal distal pulses all limbs.
Capillary refill less than 2 seconds all limbs.
Neurologic: Cranial nerves II - XII grossly intact.
Motor exam is 5/5 upper and lower extremities.
Patella tracking in the mid line.
No joint line tenderness. 1A Lockman's exam bilaterally.
Right leg examination shows that he is moderately tender over the lateral aspect of the leg with a palpable mass in the mid
He can flex and extend the
He can flex extend the ankle without difficulty.
Plain film radiographs of the tibia and fibula on the right side are reviewed.
They show an infiltrative destructive lesion in the
mid diaphysis with a transverse fracture that has some bridging callus visible.
MRI of the leg is reviewed and it shows an expansile destructive lesion with soft tissue extension involving the mid aspect of
Bone scan is reviewed and it was equivocal, moderate increase in activity in the right lower extremity.
Right fibula infiltrative destructive lesion concerning for sarcoma.
option at his age due to the neurologic complications that can occur.
In light of these facts I am recommending radical excision of the mid aspect of the fibula for diagnostic and therapeutic
but this has a very aggressive appearance for an infection with no history of prior trauma direct inoculation.
imaging studies with the patient, reviewing reports with the patient and formulating this treatment plan.
Reviewed HPI, History and ROS at today's visit.
The dictated information, contained herein, was transcribed with voice recognition software.
lidocaine HCl-hydrocortison ac (3-0.5% cream, topical) Active.
Tobacco use: Former smoker >120 years Phreesia 03/09/2026
Current work status: Not Currently Employed Phreesia 03/09/2026
Assistance Status: No Home Assistance Available Phreesia 03/09/2026
Surgical Blood Transfusion: Approves surgical blood transfusion Phreesia 03/09/2026
Drug Assessment: No Illicit Drug Use Phreesia 03/09/2026
General Not Present- Chills, Fever, Night Sweats, Persistent Infections, Recurring Infections, Weight Gain and Weight Loss.
Not Present- Burning, Decreased Memory, Difficulty Using Hands, Numbness, Seizures, Stroke and
Hematology Not Present- Easy Bruising and Excessive bleeding.
Pre-op studies to be completed by PCP
No Known Problems Status is Inactive

chartSnapshot.primary_reason_for_home_health_medical_n: The patient is considered homebound because they have a symptomatic pathologic fracture of the right fibula with an expansile, infiltrative destructive lesion and soft-tissue extension on MRI, producing moderate tenderness and aching with weightbearing (pain 7/10) that limits safe ambulation and makes exiting the home without assistance unsafe.
